acceptance_targets: [CH13-BOOKER-01] Pass if a reader can point to one explicit pre-access beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:215 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:219 that states why using Ben is dangerous and one concrete necessity that overrides that danger before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:227; fail if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241 still reads as an unprepared accident rather than the consequence of a choice or failed precaution.

[CH13-PROSE-01] Pass if each cited passage can be read as complete narration without any standalone fragment that omits the sentence's needed subject or verb for effect; fail if abstract list items like 'Contradictory assignments' or sentence shards like 'Her stipend' remain in those beats.

[CH13-PROSE-02] Pass if at least four of the cited beats no longer use either gaze-reporting or a 'not X but Y' contrast as the primary pressure cue, and each revised beat can be distinguished by a different embodied or syntactic move when read in sequence; fail if the same formulas still organize most of those moments.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can point to one explicit pre-access beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:215 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:219 that states why using Ben is dangerous and one concrete necessity that overrides that danger before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:227; fail if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241 still reads as an unprepared accident rather than the consequence of a choice or failed precaution.
findings.problem: The copy-room handoff to Ben feels mechanically arranged to give chapter 14 its leak path. The trio's fatigue is on the page, but the scene does not make the danger of letting Ben unlock scan-to-email, preview the grievance title, and accidentally receive the packet concrete enough before it happens.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:215, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:219, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:227, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241 so the group explicitly weighs the risk of Ben's proximity against a concrete deadline or technical constraint. Either make Ben's access something they knowingly accept under pressure or make him obtain it despite an on-page attempt to shut him out.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each cited passage can be read as complete narration without any standalone fragment that omits the sentence's needed subject or verb for effect; fail if abstract list items like 'Contradictory assignments' or sentence shards like 'Her stipend' remain in those beats.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly resorts to clipped fragment sentences and noun-phrase shards to manufacture urgency. In isolation they can punch; repeated across the chapter, they become mannered telegraphy and violate the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:287, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:289, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:295 into grammatically complete narrative sentences. Keep the compression, but make the pressure come from image, syntax, and causal escalation rather than from standalone labels or two-word shards.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least four of the cited beats no longer use either gaze-reporting or a 'not X but Y' contrast as the primary pressure cue, and each revised beat can be distinguished by a different embodied or syntactic move when read in sequence; fail if the same formulas still organize most of those moments.
findings.problem: Several high-pressure beats default to the same leverage markers: characters look at one another and the prose pivots through repeated 'not X but Y' contrasts. Because these formulas recur across revelation, accusation, and aftermath, distinct beats begin landing in the same rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:279, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:313, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:75, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:289 by replacing some gaze-reporting with more specific bodily or spatial reactions and recasting some contrast-template sentences into fresher syntax.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the absurdity and cruelty of graduate labor without converting the book into caricature or chase-plot menace. In edge cases, prefer the messier and more compromising version of events if it yields stronger human consequence and remains causally plausible; render sex, professional theft, bureaucratic harm, and emotional pettiness with the same specificity as any technical problem.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns vague at the exact point humiliation, erotic charge, class shame, visa fear, or institutional cruelty becomes concrete, rewrite until the cost is materially and socially legible.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High; contractions are the baseline in speech, and uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate precision, class performance, emotional emphasis, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public and private pressure, ensemble chapters and solitary ones, bureaucratic scenes and domestic scenes, deadline compression and aftermath inventory, so no two adjacent chapters rely on the same structural rhythm or dialogue density.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary English grounded in material detail; technical language should appear only when the social reality of the lab requires it, and it should never replace bodily or relational consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person centered on Leela, with occasional brief widenings that register group mood and institutional atmosphere without leaving her interpretive frame.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate compressed observational lines with longer accrual sentences that can carry comedy, shame, and procedural dread in the same movement; quiet chapters should breathe, deadline chapters should tighten without turning telegraphic.
